reasoning: France are the clear market and model favorites (roughly +164 to +188, ~33-35% on prediction markets), having won all four matches, scoring 13 and conceding just twice, with Mbappé on six goals and Olise leading assists. Their toughest hurdle is a semifinal-level gauntlet — a quarterfinal against a tough Morocco side and a likely semifinal against Spain — but their depth and clean-sheet knockout run make them the strongest pick. Argentina (defending champions) have a comparatively kind route to the last four via Egypt then Switzerland/Colombia despite an unconvincing scrape past Cape Verde, making them my runner-up, while Spain and England remain the main European threats. Norway is the live dark horse: Haaland is on a scoring rampage with three braces, they stunned Brazil in the Round of 16, but they face England in the quarterfinals.
